articles.title: Living Polymers Introduce Growth, Healing, and Regeneration to Plastics
articles.description: Researchers at Georgia Tech have developed "living" polymers capable of growing, shrinking, healing, and regenerating after fabrication. This innovation, led by Yuhang Hu, PhD, combines chemistry, mechanics, and materials science, offering potential applications in aerospace, robotics, and sustainable materials.

articles.title: Trump Revises Tariffs, Impacting Plastics Industry Imports
articles.description: The latest presidential proclamation revises Section 232 tariffs, introducing full-value treatment for derivative products, impacting costs for plastics machinery, molds, and parts. The changes include a 15% tariff on industrial equipment, a 10% levy on US-origin metal goods, and streamlined tariff assessments. Industry stakeholders must review HTS classifications and annex inclusions to ensure compliance and mitigate costs.
